title: Kootayanimoodu chamundi temple
tagline: Kootayanimoodu chamundi temple — spiritual town of Kerala

overview.short: Kootayanimoodu sri Chamundeswari temple is a Hindu temple, This ancient temple dates back to the 18th century.
overview.description: Kootayanimoodu sri Chamundeswari temple is a Hindu temple, This ancient temple dates back to the 18th century. The temple is dedicated to Chamundeswari Devi, who is a form of Durga / Shakti.The temple is located in kootayanimoodu, Vellanadu panchayat, Thiruvananthapuram district, Kerala and is one of the oldest temples in the state.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Vellanad
topPlaces.description: Vellanad is a village in the Nedumangad Taluk district of Thiruvananthapuram district in the state of Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: Vembannur
topPlaces.description: Vembannur is a village located in the Nedumangad taluk of the Thiruvananthapuram District in Kerala, India. Vembannur comes under the Aruvikkara panchayat of the Nedumangad Taluk.

topPlaces.name: Aruvikkara Dam
topPlaces.description: Aruvikkara Dam (Malayalam: അരുവിക്കര അണക്കെട്ട്) is located in Aruvikkara in Thiruvananthapuram District, Kerala. This Gravity and Masonry dam was built across the Karamana River and was completed in 1972 is used for irrigation and supplying drinking water to the city of Thiruvananthapuram.

topPlaces.name: Kuttichal
topPlaces.description: Kuttichal (formerly known as Mannoorkara) is a village in Thiruvananthapuram district in the state of Kerala, India. Kuttichal is part of Kattakada Taluk, Vellanad block panchayath and Aruvikkara Legislative Assembly.

topPlaces.name: Aruvikkara
topPlaces.description: Aruvikkara is a panchayat in Thiruvananthapuram district in the state of Kerala India. It is located on the banks of the Karamana River, 15 km from Thiruvananthapuram.

topPlaces.name: Uzhamalackal
topPlaces.description: Uzhamalackal is a village in Thiruvananthapuram district of Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: Vilappil
topPlaces.description: Vilappil is a village in Thiruvananthapuram district in the state of Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: Aryanadu
topPlaces.description: Aryanadu is a village town in Thiruvananthapuram district, Kerala, India, lying under the foothills of the Agastyarkoodam of the Sahya mountains. The village is spread along the sides of Karamana River in the western ghats of Agasthya Mountains.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Kootayanimoodu chamundi temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Kootayanimoodu chamundi temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Kootayanimoodu chamundi temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Thiruvananthapuram International Airport (17 km) and the nearest railway station is Thiruvananthapuram Central (14 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Kootayanimoodu chamundi temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Kootayanimoodu chamundi temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Kootayanimoodu chamundi temple?
faq.a: Stays in Kootayanimoodu chamundi temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Kootayanimoodu chamundi temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Vellanad, Vembannur, Aruvikkara Dam, Kuttichal, Aruvikkara.
